creator: MotionMrDave@gmail.com
text:
I am the maintainer of the other application (multimedia/motion) and FreeBSD isn't my usual distro.  

My users(and I) created a fresh install using the FreeBSD images for 13.1, installed ffmpeg (which also installs webp version 1.2.4).  We then attempted to compile the application.  It now fails due to a requirement for libsharpyuv which wasn't part of the dependencies installed via the package tool.  When a different fresh image is created and started with just doing a pkg install webp, the same result occurs.

Doing a search for packages of libsharpyuv yields no results so we are unable to resolve this new dependency.

Completing a freebsd-update fetch  install as well as a pkg update pkg upgrade did not solve the issue.

Again, FreeBSD isn't my native distro so I don't know how this process works on FreeBSD.  My expectation was that if there is a new library requirement associated with a packaged application, that library would have been installed as part of the package management system.  If this is an incorrect expectation, please just let me know.
